title: pinus pseudostrobus subsp. protuberans
intro: Pinus pseudostrobus subsp. protuberans, also known as the Tasmanian mountain pine, is a subspecies of the Pinus pseudostrobus tree, native to Tasmania. The resin of this plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Pinus pseudostrobus subsp. protuberans have been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and preventing infections. Herbal preparations of this plant often include its resin, which can be used as a topical cream, ointment, or salve to harness its medicinal properties.
